wrc_framework.definition: Weighted Response Confidence (WRC) is a quantitative metric (0.00 to 1.00) based on three component scores (E, A, P).

components.E: Empirical Evidence Score (Quantifies support from research/benchmarks).
components.A: Industry Adoption Score (Quantifies use in production MLOps/DevOps environments).
components.P: Predicted Performance Score (P_final) -- suitability for the local stack AFTER SVA Penalty Audit.

sva_violations.C1: Automation-First Operation: workflow step not executable non-interactively via CLI or API (Penalty: -0.10)
sva_violations.C2: Vendor Portability: single-vendor dependency that prevents migration within one sprint (Penalty: -0.10)
sva_violations.C3: Git-Native Traceability: artifacts not stored in version-controlled, text-diffable formats (Penalty: -0.10)
sva_violations.C4: Proportional Complexity: component cost exceeds the cost of the problem it addresses (Penalty: -0.10)
sva_violations.C5: Reuse Before Invention: duplicates logic already present in an existing, tested component (Penalty: -0.10)
sva_violations.C6: Bounded Scalability: undocumented or unfixable degradation at 10x input growth (Penalty: -0.10)

wrc_framework.p_min_production: Any solution with Final P < 0.70 after penalties is automatically classified as PoC-only. Production-ready solutions must achieve WRC >= 0.89.

p_score_audit: 1. Score P_raw (0.00-1.00) based on local stack suitability.
2. Audit the proposal against C1-C6 SVA violations.
4. Calculate Final P: P_final = P_raw - Penalty.
